subject: Re: can't import tilt series from IMOD
body:
Hi

I have the same issue, when I import to WARP it creates the tomostar file 
(it looks ok, I am using the US(English) system. But when I click to start 
processing, it does not do anything and after a while, on the graphic shell 
it gives this error (on the screenshot) and the shell gets closed while the 
WARP is still rotating its thing. Please let me know if you experienced 
such a thing or have any idea what's going on.
You can also space characters before movie names in the tomostar file, I 
edited this manually to remove the space, but the same thing happened.



Best 
Behrouz


On Monday, March 1, 2021 at 2:24:49 PM UTC-5 juha.hu...@gmail.com wrote:

> Same here. Changing from Finnish to US English solved the issue.
>
> On Friday, 26 February 2021 at 21:37:06 UTC+2 snoo...@gmail.com wrote:
>
>> Hi Alister and Julio
>>
>> Indeed changing my computer from Swedish to US English solved the issue. 
>>
>> Thanks so much for the feedback!
>>
>> Genis
>> On Friday, February 26, 2021 at 12:29:30 PM UTC+1 j.o...@fz-juelich.de 
>> wrote:
>>
>>> Hi Juha, 
>>> I had similar problems before.
>>> Indeed, it is better to change the metric system of your Windows, to the 
>>> American standard. Then decimals are separated by points and thousands by 
>>> commas.
>>> Many WARP problems just disappear after the change,
>>> Saludos,
>>> Julio
>>>
>>> Dr. Julio Ortiz
>>> EM Facility Manager
>>> Forschungszentrum Jülich GmbH
>>> Ernst Ruska-Centrum für Mikroskopie und Spektroskopie
>>> mit Elektronen
>>> ER-C-3: Strukturbiologie
>>> 52425 Jülich
>>> Germany
>>> Phone: +49 2461 61-2032 <+49%202461%20612032>
>>> Fax:       +49 2461 61-1448 <+49%202461%20611448>
>>> EMAIL: j.o...@fz-juelich.de
>>>
>>> Am 26.02.2021 um 09:08 schrieb Juha Huiskonen <juha.hu...@gmail.com>:
>>>
>>> ﻿ 
>>>
>>> Hi All,
>>>
>>> I have been able to follow the Warp tutorial to the point where the 
>>> tomostar files are created based on IMOD alignment:
>>>
>>> -  I import the tilt series and alignment information to Warp
>>>
>>> - I see the tick box appearing, indicating that alignment was found from 
>>> the IMOD folders.
>>>
>>> - The Tomostar files are created and they point to raw frames that are 
>>> in the same directory.  
>>>
>>> However, nothing happens when I click start processing (the circle 
>>> indicating the number of items to process does not appear).
>>>
>>> Any idea why this could happen and what should I do next?
>>>
>>> best wishes,
>>>
>>> Juha
>>>
>>> --
